POSSIBLE TORNADO. EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ON STALLION BLUFF ROAD. THIS COINCIDES WITH A RADAR TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE. STORM SURVEY WILL BE CONDUCTED ON THURSDAY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

MODIFIED TO EXTEND TRACK LENGTH. NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY CONFIRMED A 200 YARD WIDE EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE NORTH NORTH EAST OF SHELL KNOB NEAR STALLION BLUFF ROAD AND TRACKED NEARLY 4 MILES TO THE NORTHEAST. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST AFTER CROSSING PINEY CREEK COVE ON TABLE ROCK LAKE IN STONE COUNTY.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE GROUND SURVEY CONFIRMED A 200 YARD WIDE EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE NORTH NORTH EAST OF SHELL KNOB NEAR STALLION BLUFF ROAD AND TRACKED NEARLY 2 MILES TO THE NORTHEAST INTO MARK TWAIN NATIONAL FOREST. DUE TO TERRAIN THE SURVEY TEAM WAS UNABLE TO DETERMINE IF THE PATH EXTENDED ANY FURTHER TO THE NORTHEAST. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO DAMAGED A HOME JUST OUTSIDE OF THE SOUTHWEST EDGE OF SPRINGFIELD. DAMAGE TO THE ROOF...GUTTERS...AND AWNINGS HAD OCCURRED. THIS TORNADO WAS A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN AND THE WIDTH WAS MEASURED AT 40 YARDS. THERE WAS DEBRIS NOTED TO THE EAST OF THIS HOME...HOWEVER...IT HAS NOT BEEN DETERMINED IF THE PATH CONTINUED FURTHER.
